description_long: This is a marble bust sculpture depicting a bearded male figure with curly hair, rendered in a classical Greco-Roman style. The bust is mounted on a rectangular base, which is inscribed with the name 'GORGIA' on the front. The facial features are finely detailed, with deep-set eyes, a prominent nose, and a full beard, while the hair is intricately carved into tight curls. The back of the sculpture shows the continuation of the curly hair, with the shoulders and upper chest smoothly transitioning into the base. The surface of the sculpture appears slightly weathered, suggesting it may have been displayed outdoors or is an aged piece. The overall form is solid and monolithic, with no visible joints or separable parts.
description: A classical marble bust of a bearded man with curly hair, mounted on a rectangular base inscribed 'GORGIA'. The sculpture features detailed facial features and textured hair.
description_view_0: Front view showing the detailed face, beard, and the 'GORGIA' inscription on the base.
description_view_1: Left side view highlighting the profile of the face, the depth of the beard, and the curvature of the hair.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the intricate curls of the hair and the smooth back of the bust and base.
description_view_3: Right side view focusing on the hair texture and the profile of the face, with the base's side visible.
attribute_subject_matter: human figure, specifically a classical bust labeled 'GORGIA'
attribute_location_context: No specific context is visible; the sculpture appears isolated against a plain background.
